And here's the irony of body language. When it's in person, people start sizing you up in 1.24 of a second. They just study what it's meant to do. It's a brain machine where they can actually see what's happening as you see a new person. And they saw it boom, 1.24 of a second, up and down. Huge spike and you're down. The brain calculates that fast in body language. So when I teach people how to become effective influencers and closers, it's not about becoming some hard charging, you know, cliched salesperson. Sometimes that's called for in some industries, but more often it's not about that. More often it's about being mellow and being persuasive by using tonalities that are pleasing to the ear and that inserts subliminal messages as you're talking.
